ontology.foodon_gap_note: FoodOn has a general 'bottled water' class (FOODON_03301638); no brand-specific Voss entry exists. Closest parent used.
ontology.foodex2_gap_note: FoodEx2 AN000A covers plain drinking water as the nearest defensible category; no brand-specific Voss code exists.
ontology.snomed_ct_gap_note: SNOMED CT 11713004 represents water as a substance; used as closest parent since no bottled-water product code exists for Voss.

serving.natural_language_portion: One standard Voss still water bottle of 375 ml, equivalent to 375 g, is a typical single-serve portion as sold in restaurants and cafes.

nutrient_source.title: Water, bottled, generic (NDB No. 14555)
nutrient_source.source_match_notes: Plain still bottled water has zero macronutrients and zero calories universally. USDA SR Legacy entry for bottled water confirms all macros and energy are 0. Voss Still Water is natural artesian still water with no additives, consistent with this profile.

gi_source.title: Glycemic index of water and non-caloric beverages — University of Sydney GI Database convention
gi_source.source_match_notes: Pure still water contains no glycemic carbohydrates and does not raise blood glucose. The University of Sydney GI Database does not assign a formal GI to water as it contains no carbohydrates. By established convention, water is assigned GI = 1 (the lowest valid positive sentinel value) and gi_category = 'low'. Glycemic load per serving is 0.0 because carbohydrate_g = 0.

hypertension.guidance: Voss Still Water contains no sodium and is entirely safe for individuals with hypertension; adequate daily water intake supports blood pressure regulation and renal function.

type2_diabetes.key_factor: Zero carbohydrates and zero glycemic load
type2_diabetes.guidance: Voss Still Water contains no carbohydrates or sugars and has a glycemic load of zero, making it the optimal beverage choice for individuals managing Type 2 Diabetes.

cardiovascular_disease.key_factor: Zero fat, zero sodium, zero calories
cardiovascular_disease.guidance: Voss Still Water contains no saturated fat, trans fat, sodium, or calories, and is fully appropriate for individuals with cardiovascular disease as part of adequate daily hydration.

llm_training.natural_language_description: Voss Still Water is a premium Norwegian natural artesian still water sourced from an underground aquifer in Iveland, Norway, protected by layers of rock and ice that filter the water naturally over many years. It is bottled at the source without carbonation, additives, or treatment beyond filtration, resulting in a clean, neutral taste with a very low mineral content. Voss is widely recognised for its distinctive cylindrical glass and PET bottle design. It contains zero calories, zero macronutrients, zero sodium, and zero sugar, making it nutritionally inert and universally suitable for all dietary patterns. In clinical nutrition, still water is the gold-standard beverage for individuals managing Type 2 Diabetes, hypertension, cardiovascular disease, and obesity, as it contributes to hydration without any glycemic, caloric, or sodium load. In Singapore, Voss Still Water is sold in restaurants, cafes, and food delivery platforms as a premium bottled water option, typically priced above commodity bottled water brands. It is suitable for vegetarians, vegans, halal, kosher, and gluten-free diets.
common_user_queries: Is Voss water good for diabetics?
Does Voss still water have calories?
What is the sodium content of Voss water?
Is Voss water suitable for hypertension?
Can I drink Voss water on a diet?
What is the GI of Voss water?

confidence_reason: Voss Still Water is plain still water with no additives. USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy confirms all macronutrients and energy are zero for bottled still water. This is a universally consistent and well-established nutritional profile for pure water. No estimation was required for core macros.
notes: energy_kcal is naturally zero as pure still water contains no caloric macronutrients. protein_g is naturally zero as water contains no protein. fat_g is naturally zero as water contains no fat. carbohydrate_g is naturally zero as water contains no carbohydrates. All values confirmed zero per USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy for bottled water. glycemic_index set to 1 (effectively zero) as this beverage contains no glycemic carbohydrates; gi_category low per convention. glycemic_load_per_serving is 0.0 since carbohydrate_g is 0. Sodium is reported as 0.0 mg per 100g for Voss Still Water per product label and USDA reference; trace minerals may vary by batch but are below detection threshold for nutritional reporting. The cuisine tag 'Taiwanese' from the input source (field survey data) reflects the restaurant context (Riverside Buns - Boat Quay) and is not a property of the food itself; cuisine has been corrected to 'Global / International' and region_of_origin set to 'Norway'. All -1 sentinel values have been replaced with 0.0 for this food as water universally contains none of these nutrients and confirmed zero values are appropriate. monounsaturated_fat_g, polyunsaturated_fat_g, and trans_fat_g are confirmed zero for pure water. All vitamin and mineral fields are confirmed zero for pure still water per USDA SR Legacy.

Set glycemic_index to 1 (sentinel positive value) and gi_category to 'low' per zero-calorie beverage glycemic rule.
